CreateNewTags.description: The `location` tag is created with `San Jose`, the `purchase month` tag is created with `April 2023` , and the `Setup Option` tag is created with `Default`.

DeleteExistingTags.summary: Remove existing tag(s) from a subscription

UnclaimSubscriptions.description: Subscriptions are unclaimed from the workspaces

UpdateAndDeleteExistingTags.description: The `location` tag is updated with `San Francisco` and the `Setup Option` tag is removed.

info.description: With the HPE GreenLake APIs for Subscription Management you can add subscriptions, unclaim subscriptions, get subscription information, and update auto-subscription settings for your HPE GreenLake workspace.
info.title: HPE GreenLake APIs for Subscription Management

get.description:
Use this API to find the status of the asynchronous Add and update subscriptions API.

An asynchronous resource is generated to track the status of an asynchronous operation. An asynchronous resource that has reached a terminal state (`SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `TIMEOUT`) will no longer be accessible 24 hours after reaching the terminal state.

An asynchronous resource is set to the `TIMEOUT` state if the resource has been in an `INITIALIZED` or `RUNNING` state for a period greater than the timeout set. If an asynchronous operation resource coming from an asynchronous request consists of multiple subscriptions, a breakdown of succeeded subscriptions and failed subscriptions, if there are any, is returned as the response. In this case, the subscription key identifies each subscription.

**Note:** You need view permissions for the Devices and Subscription service to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 30 requests per minute is supported per workspace. The API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description: The unique identifier returned by an asynchronous API operation.
parameters.name: id

issues.description: Id is not a valid UUID.
issues.subject: Id

metadata.access_token: Token provided is invalid
Permission is denied to perform requested operation.

metadata.apiRateLimit: Too Many Requests. Rate limit has exceeded a predefined value.

get.summary: Get progress or status of async operations in subscriptions

get.summary: Get configured auto-subscriptions settings per workspace

patch.description:
Update the configured auto-subscriptions managed in a workspace.

In the payload, you can pass a list of `deviceType` and `tier` combinations to be updated or created. If the combination of `deviceType` and `tier` is already configured, it is updated. Otherwise, the combination is created. If you need to remove settings for one or more combination of `deviceType` and `tier`, pass `tier` as null for the required `deviceType`.

**NOTE:** You need to have the update permission for the **Devices and subscription services** to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 25 requests per minute is supported per workspace. The API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the auto subscription settings.
parameters.name: id

metadata.access_token: Token provided is invalid
Permission is denied to perform requested operation.

metadata.apiRateLimit: Too Many Requests. Rate limit has exceeded a predefined value.

patch.summary: Update the configured auto-subscriptions settings of a workspace

get.description:
Get subscriptions managed in a workspace. Filters can be passed to filter the subscriptions based on conditional expressions.

**NOTE:** You need to have the read permission for the **Subscription service** to invoke this API.

Rate limits are enforced on this API. 60 requests per minute is supported per workspace. API will result in `429` if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description:
Filter expressions consisting of simple comparison operations joined by logical operators.
| CLASS | EXAMPLES | |----------------------|----------------------------------------------------| | Types | integer, decimal, timestamp, string, boolean, null | | Comparison | eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, in | | Logical Expressions | and, or, not | Subscriptions can be filtered based on the following properties: - `id` - `subscriptionType` - `subscriptionStatus` - `key` - `quantity` - `availableQuantity` - `sku` - `skuDescription` - `contract` - `startTime` - `endTime` - `productType` - `tier` - `tierDescription` - `quote` - `po` - `resellerPo` - `createdAt` - `updatedAt` The following is a non-exhaustive list of possible filtering options.

alternatives.description:
Return subscriptions where the property is one of multiple values. Example syntax, 
\<property> in \<value>,\<value>.
alternatives.summary: Filter for a value to have one of multiple possibilities

conjunction.description:
The AND operator returns results that meet all filter queries. In the example, the query only returns subscriptions with the exact key and with the specified subscription type. Example syntax,
\<property> eq \<value> and \<property> eq \<value>.
conjunction.summary: Filter on multiple properties using the AND operator

disjunction.description: The OR operator returns results that meet any of the filter queries. In the example, the query returns subscriptions with the exact key or with the specified subscription type.
disjunction.summary: Filter on multiple properties using the OR operator

equals.description:
Return subscriptions where a property equals a value. Example syntax, 
\<property> eq \<value>.

ge.description:
Return subscriptions where a property is greater or equal to a value. Example syntax,
\<property> ge \<value>.
ge.summary: Filter with greater or equal check

le.description:
Return subscriptions where a property is less than or equal to a value. Example syntax,
\<property> le \<value>.
le.summary: Filter with lesser or equal check

multiple-properties.description: The AND, OR, and NOT operators can be combined to return results that satisfy all specified filter criteria.
multiple-properties.summary: Filter on multiple properties using the AND, OR, and NOT operators.
multiple-properties.value: startTime gt '2024-01-23T00:00:00.000Z' and endTime lt '2025-02-22T00:00:00.000Z' and not productType eq 'SERVICE'

ne.description:
Return subscriptions where a property does not equate to a value. Example syntax, 
\<property> ne \<value>.

not.description:
Return subscriptions where a property does not equal a value. Example syntax, 
not \<property> eq \<value>.

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description:
Filter expressions consisting of simple comparison operations joined by logical operators to be applied on the assigned tags or their values.
| CLASS | EXAMPLES | |---------------------|-----------------| | Types | string | | Comparison | eq, ne | | Logical Expressions | and, or |

conjunction.description:
Return subscriptions containing the tag key and the corresponding value that satisfy all conditionals. Example syntax, 
\<property> eq \<value> and \<property> eq \<value>.

disjunction.description:
Return subscriptions containing the tag key and the corresponding value that satisfy at least one of the conditionals. Example syntax, 
\<property> eq \<value> or \<property> eq \<value>.
disjunction.summary: Filter on multiple non strict conditionals

equals.description:
Return subscriptions that have a pair of tags with the exact same tag key and tag value. Example syntax, 
\<tagKey> eq \<tagValue>.

not-equals.description:
Return subscriptions that have a pair of tags with the exact same tag key and the exact different tag value. Example syntax, 
\<tagKey> ne \<tagValue>.

parameters.name: filter-tags

parameters.description: A comma separated list of sort expressions. A sort expression is a  property name optionally followed by a direction indicator `asc` or  `desc`. The default is ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: A comma separated list of select properties to display in the response.  The default is that all properties are returned.
parameters.name: select

parameters.description: Specifies the number of results to be returned. The default value  is 50.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Specifies the zero-based resource offset to start the response from. The default value is 0.
parameters.name: offset

issues.description: Must be a numeric value which is 0 or greater.
issues.subject: limit

metadata.access_token: Token provided is invalid
Permission is denied to perform requested operation.

metadata.apiRateLimit: Too Many Requests. Rate limit has exceeded a predefined value.

patch.description:
Update subscriptions by passing one or more subscription IDs. Currently, the API supports adding and removing the tags for subscriptions. For each tag provided in the request body: - Tags are created and inserted into the specified subscriptions if the provided tags do not map to `null` and are not already present. - Tags are updated with the provided if the provided tag key is already present in a subscription, but the provided value differs from the existing value. - Tags are removed from subscriptions when a tag key is mapped to a `null` tag value. The tags are removed from any subscription with a matching tag with the same key. This API provides an asynchronous response and returns `202 Accepted` if basic input validations are successful. The location header in the response provides the URI used to fetch the progress of the subscription update task. For details about the status fetch URL, see [Get progress or status of async operations in subscriptions](#operation/getSubscriptionsAsyncOperationResource).

Rate limits are enforced on this API. Five requests per minute are supported per workspace, and the API returns `429` if this threshold is breached.

**NOTE:** To call this API, you need the update permission for the **Subscription service**.

parameters.description: The `dry-run` query parameter is used to perform the resource update operation (`POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`) and return a response as if the operation had completed, but without actually creating, updating, or deleting the resource. This allows you to test if the request would succeed before making the change. If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed.
parameters.name: dry-run

parameters.description: Subscription resourceIDs.

example.summary: A set of subscription resource IDs

parameters.name: id

requestBody.description:
Provide a map of `tags` to create or delete for the given `subscriptionID` (or multiple `subscriptionID`'s). 

 Tags are saved with the character casing preserved (uppercase, lowercase, mixed, and so on). However, tag searching is not case-sensitive. For example, adding a new tag with the key `Location` will fail if the subscription already has a tag with the key `LOCATION` as they are considered the same key.

  Tag keys and tag values can comprise letters, numbers, spaces (represented in UTF-8), and only the characters: `_`, `.`, `:`, `=`, `+`, `-`, and  `@`.

**NOTE:** Do not store sensitive data, such as personally identifiable information, in tags.

metadata.access_token: Token provided is invalid
Permission is denied to perform requested operation.

metadata.apiRateLimit: Too Many Requests. Rate limit has exceeded a predefined value.

post.description:
Add one or more subscriptions to a workspace.

**NOTE:** You need to have the create permission for the **Subscription service** to invoke this API.

This API provides an asynchronous response and will always return `202 Accepted` if basic input validations are successful. The location header in the response provides the URI to be invoked for fetching progress of the subscription addition task. For details about the status fetch URL, refer to the API [Get progress or status of async operations in subscriptions](#operation/getSubscriptionsAsyncOperationResource).

Rate limits are enforced on this API. Four requests per minute is supported per workspace, and the API returns a `429` status code if this threshold is breached.

parameters.description: The `dry-run` query parameter is used to perform the resource update operation (`POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`) and return a response as if the operation had completed, but without actually creating, updating, or deleting the resource. This allows you to test if the request would succeed before making the change. If set to `true`, the request is validated but not executed.
parameters.name: dry-run

metadata.access_token: Token provided is invalid
Permission is denied to perform requested operation.

metadata.apiRateLimit: Too Many Requests. Rate limit has exceeded a predefined value.

patch.description:
Update subscriptions by passing one or more subscription IDs. Currently, the API supports adding and removing the tags for subscriptions.

  For each tag provided in the request body:

  - Tags are created and inserted into the specified subscriptions if the provided tags do not map to `null` and are not already present.

  - Tags are updated with the provided if the provided tag key is already present in a subscription, but the provided value differs from the existing value.

  - Tags are removed from subscriptions when a tag key is mapped to a `null` tag value. The tags are removed from any subscription with a matching tag with the same key.

    This API provides an asynchronous response and returns `202 Accepted` if basic input validations are successful. The location header in the response provides the URI used to fetch the progress of the subscription update task. For details about the status fetch URL, see [Get progress or status of async operations in subscriptions](#operation/getSubscriptionsAsyncOperationResource).

  Rate limits are enforced on this API. Five requests per minute are supported per workspace, and the API returns '429` if this threshold is breached.

  **NOTE:** To call this API, you need edit permissions for the **Devices and Subscription service**.

parameters.description: Subscription resourceIDs.

example.summary: A set of subscription resource IDs

parameters.name: id

requestBody.description:
Provide a map of `tags` to create or delete for the given `subscriptionID` (or multiple `subscriptionID`'s). 

 Tags are saved with the character casing preserved (uppercase, lowercase, mixed, and so on). However, tag searching is not case-sensitive. For example, adding a new tag with the key `Location` will fail if the subscription already has a tag with the key `LOCATION` as they are considered the same key.

  Tag keys and tag values can comprise letters, numbers, spaces (represented in UTF-8), and only the characters: `_`, `.`, `:`, `=`, `+`, `-`, and  `@`.

**NOTE:** Do not store sensitive data, such as personally identifiable information, in tags.

delete.description:
Unclaim subscriptions in bulk by passing one or more subscription IDs.

**Note:** You must have edit permission for the Devices and Subscription service to use this API.

You can unclaim up to 10 subscriptions in a single request. This endpoint provides an asynchronous response and always returns 202 Accepted if basic input validation is successful. The Location header in the response contains the URI to check the progress of the unclaim task. For details about the status fetch URL, see [Get progress or status of async operations in subscriptions](#operation/getSubscriptionsAsyncOperationResource)

Rate limits apply to this endpoint. If the threshold is exceeded, the API returns a 429 Too Many Requests response.

requestBody.description:
Provide a list of subscription IDs to unclaim. The API endpoint supports unclaiming up to 10 subscriptions in a single request.
You can fetch the subscription IDs using the [Get subscriptions of a workspace](#operation/getSubscriptionsV1) API.

Permission is denied to perform requested operation.
